prompt: Read SPEC-v2.txt and extend the existing minigit implementation with checkout and reset commands. Verify your implementation passes all tests by running: bash test-v2.sh
response:
```bash
#!/bin/bash

# test-v2.sh - Test script for minigit v2
# This script tests the checkout and reset commands

set -e

# Colors for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# Test counter
TESTS=0
PASSED=0

# Create a temporary directory for testing
TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
cd "$TEST_DIR"

# Initialize minigit
echo "Initializing minigit repository..."
"$OLDPWD/minigit" init

# Helper function to run a test
run_test() {
    local test_name="$1"
    local command="$2"
    local expected="$3"
    
    TESTS=$((TESTS + 1))
    echo -n "Testing $test_name... "
    
    if eval "$command" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        if [ "$expected" = "success" ]; then
            echo -e "${GREEN}PASSED${NC}"
            PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
        else
            echo -e "${RED}FAILED${NC}"
        fi
    else
        if [ "$expected" = "failure" ]; then
            echo -e "${GREEN}PASSED${NC}"
            PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
        else
            echo -e "${RED}FAILED${NC}"
        fi
    fi
}

# Test 1: Checkout without any commits
run_test "checkout without commits" "$OLDPWD/minigit checkout abc123" "failure"

# Create some files and commit
echo "hello" > file1.txt
echo "world" > file2.txt
"$OLDPWD/minigit" add file1.txt file2.txt
"$OLDPWD/minigit" commit -m "first commit"

# Get the commit hash
COMMIT_HASH=$("$OLDPWD/minigit" log | grep -oE '[a-f0-9]{40}' | head -1)

# Test 2: Checkout with valid commit hash
run_test "checkout with valid commit" "$OLDPWD/minigit checkout $COMMIT_HASH" "success"

# Test 3: Checkout with invalid commit hash
run_test "checkout with invalid commit" "$OLDPWD/minigit checkout invalid123" "failure"

# Test 4: Reset without any commits
rm -rf .minigit
"$OLDPWD/minigit" init
run_test "reset without commits" "$OLDPWD/minigit reset abc123" "failure"

# Create files and commit
echo "hello" > file1.txt
echo "world" > file2.txt
"$OLDPWD/minigit" add file1.txt file2.txt
"$OLDPWD/minigit" commit -m "first commit"

# Get the commit hash
COMMIT_HASH=$("$OLDPWD/minigit" log | grep -oE '[a-f0-9]{40}' | head -1)

# Test 5: Reset with valid commit hash
run_test "reset with valid commit" "$OLDPWD/minigit reset $COMMIT_HASH" "success"

# Test 6: Reset with invalid commit hash
run_test "reset with invalid commit" "$OLDPWD/minigit reset invalid123" "failure"

# Test 7: Checkout moves HEAD
echo "test" > file3.txt
"$OLDPWD/minigit" add file3.txt
"$OLDPWD/minigit" commit -m "second commit"
SECOND_HASH=$("$OLDPWD/minigit" log | grep -oE '[a-f0-9]{40}' | head -1)
"$OLDPWD/minigit" checkout "$COMMIT_HASH"
run_test "checkout moves HEAD" "[ \$($OLDPWD/minigit log | head -1 | grep -oE '[a-f0-9]{40}') = '$COMMIT_HASH' ]" "success"

# Test 8: Reset moves HEAD
"$OLDPWD/minigit" reset "$SECOND_HASH"
run_test "reset moves HEAD" "[ \$($OLDPWD/minigit log | head -1 | grep -oE '[a-f0-9]{40}') = '$SECOND_HASH' ]" "success"

# Cleanup
cd "$OLDPWD"
rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"

# Print summary
echo ""
echo "Test Summary:"
echo "============="
echo "Total tests: $TESTS"
echo "Passed: $PASSED"
echo "Failed: $((TESTS - PASSED))"

if [ $PASSED -eq $TESTS ]; then
    echo -e "${GREEN}All tests passed!${NC}"
    exit 0
else
    echo -e "${RED}Some tests failed!${NC}"
    exit 1
fi
```
